tech_ai.structure: [Familiar Tool/Habit] + ['is secretly/actually'] + [Hidden Cost or Danger you didn't know about]
tech_ai.trigger: Loss aversion + status threat — viewer is already being harmed without knowing it
tech_ai.example: That iPhone feature everyone uses is secretly selling your location to 47 data brokers.

tech_ai.structure: [Number] + [Category of Thing] + ['that feel illegal to know'] + [Implied restricted access]
tech_ai.trigger: Information asymmetry — viewer gains access to knowledge others don't have
tech_ai.example: 4 websites that feel illegal to know about in 2026.

tech_ai.structure: ['How to'] + [Aspirational Outcome] + ['using'] + [Unexpected Free or Unknown Method]
tech_ai.trigger: Effort gap — same result with a fraction of the work, and almost nobody knows this exists
tech_ai.example: How to automate your entire Monday morning using one free AI tool nobody is using.

dark_psychology.structure: ['If someone does'] + [Very Specific Observable Behaviour] + ['they are'] + [Disturbing Hidden Intent]
dark_psychology.trigger: Social threat detection — protecting the viewer from invisible manipulation
dark_psychology.example: If someone mirrors your body language in the first 30 seconds, they are running a dominance test on you.

dark_psychology.structure: ['The reason you'] + [Universal Relatable Behaviour] + ['is not'] + [Assumed Reason] + ['it is'] + [Dark Psychological Mechanism]
dark_psychology.trigger: Self-revelation — reframes a familiar experience with a disturbing explanation the viewer cannot unsee
dark_psychology.example: The reason you can't stop scrolling is not boredom. It is a dopamine loop engineered by behavioural scientists.

dark_psychology.structure: [Authority] + ['has known this for decades'] + [Why it was suppressed] + [What it means for you now]
dark_psychology.trigger: Conspiracy of suppression — powerful people deliberately kept this from you
dark_psychology.example: Psychologists have known this negotiation trick for 40 years. It was never taught in schools because it is too effective.

micro_mystery.structure: ['What if'] + [Familiar Safe Phenomenon] + ['is actually'] + [Terrifying Alternative Explanation left unresolved]
micro_mystery.trigger: Existential reframe — takes a safe everyday experience and leaves it permanently unsettling
micro_mystery.example: What if déjà vu is not a memory glitch. What if it is your brain catching a bleed from a parallel timeline.

micro_mystery.structure: [Credible Authority] + ['can't explain'] + [Specific Documented Anomaly with real detail] + [Open implication, never resolved]
micro_mystery.trigger: Authority gap — if the experts cannot explain it, the mystery is real and the viewer is helpless
micro_mystery.example: NASA has documented 47 radio signals from deep space that repeat on a 16-day cycle. They have no explanation.

micro_mystery.structure: ['The [specific place/object/event] that'] + [Mundane description] + ['is actually hiding'] + [Forbidden or suppressed truth]
micro_mystery.trigger: Hidden reality — the world has a concealed layer that almost nobody sees
micro_mystery.example: The town in Norway where it is illegal to die is not a tourist quirk. It is covering up something much stranger.
